commit.message:
Phase 2 完成：conflict detection、compact、create_if_useful、search_context、eval_metrics

- conflict detection: FTS OR 搜尋 + 40% 有效詞重疊判定衝突
- compact(): 移除過期記憶，回傳 CompactResult 統計
- create_if_useful(): 結合 admission + importance 門檻 + 衝突偵測
- add_with_result(): 回傳 AddResult 含 was_duplicate + conflicts
- search_context(): 給 agent 的格式化 context 字串
- eval_metrics(): 查詢數、記憶數、平均延遲、過期/stale 統計
- store.search_any(): OR 邏輯 FTS5 搜尋
- 新增 AddResult / CompactResult / EvalMetrics 資料模型
- MemoryRecord.conflict_ids 欄位
- 20 個新測試，全套 184 tests 通過

fix: new launch signals now require positive momentum and sub-$50M cap

Previously the filter showed any token with >50% turnover regardless of
price direction — a token being dumped (300% turnover, -40% 24h) looked
identical to a token being discovered. Now requires at least one positive
momentum signal (1h or 24h > 0) and market cap < $50M to eliminate large
caps with incidentally high volume. Clarified context label to Claude.

commit.message:
fix(e2e): handle exit test session invalidation after window close

Exit tests typed "exit" which closed the Tauri window and invalidated
the WebDriver session. Subsequent tests/checks failed with "invalid
session id". Fixed by:
- Reordering non-destructive tests before destructive ones
- Wrapping post-exit operations in try/catch
- Replacing window.ptyClient with correct window.tabManager API
- Removing pty_exit listener test (dynamic import unsupported)

Task 39: Rename logger parameter to match spec (#39)

* Task 39: refactor: rename logger param warnEnabled to debugEnabled

Rename the `createLogger` parameter from `warnEnabled` to `debugEnabled`
to match the Task 5 spec. Update test description accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: clarify debugEnabled JSDoc to explain debug-mode semantics

The parameter maps to config.debug (data-debug attr). When debug mode
is active, messages are promoted from console.debug to console.warn.
Updated JSDoc to explain this two-tier logging relationship clearly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Security hardening: canAccessPanel, CSP, rate limiting, session flags

- User::canAccessPanel(): restrict to @somaticdance.space emails (was open to all users)
- SurveyToken: remove 'used_at' from fillable (prevents mass-assignment DoS)
- nginx: add Content-Security-Policy header
- nginx: repeat security headers in /build/ and static asset location blocks
  (nginx add_header inheritance is cancelled when child blocks define their own headers)
- routes: add throttle middleware (30/min on /s/{token}, 60/min on /{slug})
- .env.production: add SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE=true, SESSION_ENCRYPT=true

bigsmile, wow, shocked (wide eyes), sleepy (eyes closed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial release: org-agent.el — orchestrate AI agents via org-mode

Extracted from the Bandit project into a standalone Emacs package.

org-agent binds AI agent sessions to org-mode headings. Each heading
is one agent session. Parent headings plan and decompose; child headings
execute in isolated git worktrees. State transitions (TODO → NEXT → DONE)
trigger auto-commit, merge, and cascading dispatch.

Features:
- Recursive task decomposition via org tree
- Git worktree isolation per NEXT agent
- Auto-commit on DONE, auto-merge on parent DONE
- Sequential chains for data-dependent children
- Inter-agent messaging via emacsclient
- Multiple backend support (Claude, Codex, z.ai)
- PreToolUse hook scripts for edit/commit guards
- Configurable formatters, paths, methodology text
- Backward-compatible claude-org-* aliases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: org-agent
repository.description: Orchestrate AI agents via org-mode headings with git worktree isolation

Initial commit: Ronin landing page base

- Cloned from Palantir All-in-One Next.js 15 template
- Added comprehensive README with project overview
- 12-section landing page structure (MAP framework)
- Palantir-inspired design system (dark theme, enterprise)
- Tech stack: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind, Framer Motion

🤖 Agent orchestration system defined:
- CCO Ogilvy: Content strategy & copywriting
- CTO Musk: Technical architecture & optimization
- Orchestrator: Integration & deployment

🎯 Target: B2B SaaS landing page for AI agent management

native HTMLInputElement value setter + input/change events to properly
update React state.

commit.message:
fix(add): continue on errors and report all failures at end (#191)

* fix(add): continue on errors and report all failures at end

Previously, `portolan add .` stopped immediately on the first file that
failed, leaving other valid files unprocessed. This change implements
Issue #175 by:

- Continuing to process all files even when some fail
- Collecting all errors in an AddFailure dataclass
- Reporting all failures at the end with a summary
- Exiting with non-zero code if any failures occurred

Example output:
  Added 5 items
  2 items failed:
    - censo-2010/data.parquet: missing bounding box
    - censo-2022/data.parquet: missing bounding box

Also refactors _output_add_results into smaller helper functions to
satisfy xenon complexity limits.

Closes #175

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(add): address PR #191 review feedback

Bug fixes:
- Update stale comment in ClickException handler that contradicted
  the new continue-on-errors behavior (said "should propagate" when
  errors are now recorded as failures)
- Standardize error message format: store only str(err) in
  AddFailure.error for all exception types, avoiding redundant
  path duplication in output
- Remove unused collection_id parameter from _output_add_results
- Add try/except around deferred non-geo file processing so
  _copy_non_geo_to_item_dir and _update_item_with_asset errors
  don't abort the batch
- Add catch-all Exception handler after ClickException and
  ValueError/FileNotFoundError to prevent unexpected error types
  from aborting the entire batch
- Fix misleading backward compatibility test: rename from
  test_existing_call_sites_work_with_two_value_unpack to
  test_add_files_returns_three_value_tuple since the test
  actually verifies the new 3-tuple contract

Test improvements:
- Add test for zero successes + some skipped + some failures
  in human output
- Add end-to-end test for ClickException error formatting
  (verifies no path duplication)
- Add test for JSON output with zero successes and only failures
- Remove unused TYPE_CHECKING imports from test files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): use cross-platform path separator in assertion

Windows uses backslashes in path strings, so the hardcoded
forward-slash path 'census/data.parquet' doesn't match the actual
output on Windows ('census\data.parquet').

Fix by using f-string with the Path object directly, which will use
the correct platform-specific separator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(catalog): filter versioning & grouped catalog (#265)

## Summary

- **DB migration**: adds `introduced_at`, `deprecated_at`,
`successor_hash` columns to `filters` table for version tracking
- **Grouped catalog**: new `catalog/grouped.json` R2 artifact that
merges filters by normalized command pattern (e.g. `gh issue view *` and
`gh issue view` are grouped together), with primary filter selection
(current stdlib preferred)
- **New endpoints**: `GET /api/catalog/grouped` (bearer auth) and `POST
/api/filters/backfill-versions` (service auth)
- **Publish-stdlib enhancements**: tracks `introduced_at` version, runs
deprecation sweep to mark removed/replaced stdlib filters
- **Backfill CLI**: `tokf backfill-versions` command reconstructs
version history from git tags (gated behind `stdlib-publish` feature)
- **CI workflow**: `backfill-versions.yml` (workflow_dispatch) for
one-shot historical backfill

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for grouping logic, primary selection, wildcard
normalization, serde round-trips
- [x] Unit tests for version info serialization (None fields omitted)
- [x] Unit tests for R2 write functions (in-memory storage mock)
- [x] Unit tests for backfill semver comparison and timeline computation
- [x] DB integration tests: insert filters with version data, verify
grouped catalog query
- [x] E2E: publish-stdlib with version → verify deprecation sweep
- [ ] Manual: run `tokf backfill-versions --dry-run` against repo

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix UI freeze on large configs via lazy block parsing (#95)

Make block element types (BLOCK_STMT, MAP_BLOCK_STMT, etc.) lazy-parseable
using ILazyParseableElementType so BlockSupportImpl only reparses the
changed block instead of the entire file, preventing DiffTree.build
stack overflow.

- Add NginxLazyBlockElementType extending ILazyParseableElementType
- Add NginxElementTypeFactory to route block types to lazy element type
- Add NginxLazyBlockParser overriding parse_root_ for block dispatch
- Update Nginx.bnf with elementTypeFactory attribute
- Bump version to 2026.1.10
